how you can reduce your stress and make you more productive in life, how you can lose weight. So repositioning his offer is going to drastically change his business. We're going from very features-oriented to more opportunity-oriented. Here's another in the same niche as what Ken Roberts would have been, very hardcore business opportunity looking for opportunity seekers. His name is Russ Dalby. You may have seen him on TV. He does a lot of infomercials. Russ sells a real estate product on how to buy and sell real estate notes. In his headline here, it says, I made a million dollars the hard way but you don't have to. He's going to go on and he's going to tell a story, which is going to diffuse this whole real estate issue right now. He's going to go on and tell a story of saying, I've made a million dollars. I'm going to take you by the hand. I'm going to let you look over my shoulder and see and do exactly what I do, so you can make money from it too. Sound familiar? On the front, we're going to appeal to the opportunity seeker. We're going to talk about current issues, banks abandoned 169 billion markets, how to claim your share in this once in a lifetime moneymaker before it's too late. A couple other headlines on here, how to legally rob banks blind. Doesn't that sound good? American dreams come true, millions made in the deserted market. Mortgage meltdown creates huge profit potential for the informed giving them all the reasons to read it. We throw Gary Collins on the back, throw some celebrity in there, it's a very effective sales piece. Melt tens of millions of pieces for Rust Albee. This is my favorite one that I want to talk about today. How many of you guys think software is exciting? One or two people, three people in here, and I agree, I'm not a software fan. I think software is the most boring thing to talk about. One of my clients, Reg online, they are a meeting planner software, not very exciting at all. If you were to go to the Boston Marathon, they would be their software that's being used to register people for the event, signing them up, putting their names in a database, processing the credit card, whatever it is. When I started working with them, they were mailing this piece here. So as they're introducing a meeting planners favorite four-letter word, easy. They go on to say, Reg online makes everything about your event registration easier with automated follow-up emails, very feature oriented. One click summer reports, feature oriented. And instant payment processing, very feature oriented. I changed them to this piece here. For your eyes only, the event planner's secret weapon. Discover how to cut your cost, event cost up to 25% while increasing attendance up to 20%. Increase your total registrants, increase your attendee satisfaction, improve your market penetration, and on and on and on. Do you guys see the difference between the two sales pieces? So the first sales piece, when I started working with them, they're mailing 20,000 pieces a year. I'm now mailing 850,000 pieces a year for them. See, there's a huge difference just by changing the way we present to the offer. There's one thing on here that could make a major change in this increase the response even more. It's something I just learned this weekend here. You guys see that word right there? Improve. So if I wanted to take this to the next level, what do I need to do? That's right, make it more opportunity oriented. So I've just discovered a way that I can take a client who's been mailing 850,000 pieces a year, and either increase the response or increase the universe by making the change. So the way we present our offers is key to growth in your business. Here is probably the most interesting piece I'll share with you. This guy, I can't even pronounce his last name. I don't know if he can pronounce it. He told me when I started working with him to call him Dr. Batman. Dr. Batman, yes, that's right, Dr. Batman. He is Iranian. He's a doctor. He got thrown in an Iranian prison for political reasons. And while he was in prison, he was the only doctor there. And so he had to learn, and everyone, all the patients, not all the patients, all the prisoners came to him with sicknesses and ailments, and they were all asking his advice because he was a doctor. And so he had to learn or find a way to heal these patients. And the only thing he had to work with was water. So he found the healing properties of water, how to properly use it. And so he dedicated his life to the research on how to use water to improve your health. So he came up with the God Send Water Cure Program. Now, in this letter, I think it's a 12-page letter, if we told people to go out and drink water, do you think anybody would buy the course? No. No, they're going to go over the tap, they're going to fill up a glass, and they're going to take a drink. So instead, we told people about how when you want to drink, if you want to use water as a healing property for yourself, you need to drink it at certain intervals. There are certain times during the day that if you drink water at these times, it can help you lose weight. There are certain quantities of water that you need to drink at certain times in order to prevent ulcers or a common existing ulcer. There's healing properties to the amounts of water you drink at certain intervals for curing back pain. So a whole list of things. So we didn't tell them just go drink water. So we said buy this course, and we will show you how to properly drink it so it can change and have an impact on your health. I handed out a piece to everyone here. It's the greatest business on earth. So when Ken retired from selling a commodity course, he had made a couple hundred million dollars and decided to retire. I don't know why, but he just decided he was tired of working. And he sold off a small portion of it, the US Tart Company, and this is what's left today. And I still do mailings for them. We mailed out about 100,000 pieces a little over a week ago, and I asked if I could ship everyone here a copy. Because this is a great example of a business opportunity piece to opportunity seekers that's working in the mail today. And there's some things I want to show you in it that's very important to the way the offer is presented. The first is this is for a commodity option course. If you were to go and read this 56-page digest, you would not know that we're selling you a commodity option course until you're at least halfway through it. You'd have to be about page 24 or 25 before you realize what we were selling you. Why is that? Because when you say the word commodities, people have a preconceived idea about it.
